title: Permissionless DNS
tagline: Decentralised name resolution for Logos services.
summary: A naming layer for Logos apps that does not depend on ICANN, registrars, or any single authority you can subpoena.
description: Domain names are still the bottleneck where governments and registrars exert pressure on otherwise decentralised projects. Permissionless DNS is a long-running idea — ENS, Handshake, etc. — but no implementation has yet wired cleanly into the Logos App. This idea proposes a Logos-native resolution layer with cache attestation, multi-root trust, and graceful fallback to traditional DNS for compatibility, so users can reach Logos services even when their resolver is hostile.
